Safety Mar 30, 2026

Video: Delta A330 returns to São Paulo after engine failure on takeoff

A Delta Air Lines Airbus A330-300 returned to São Paulo Guarulhos Airport shortly after takeoff on March 29,…...

Engine failures during critical takeoff phase remain rare enough that successful returns to departure airports demonstrate modern aircraft redundancy and crew training effectiveness.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Why Airlines Don’t Always Use Their Newest Jets

Examining why the three US legacy carriers operate relatively old aircraft, and why American Airlines' fleet is yoinger than the rest.

Legacy carriers balance depreciation costs and fuel efficiency against existing aircraft investment, making fleet age decisions driven by financial optimization rather than pure modernity.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Artemis II by the numbers: an inside look at mankind’s planned return to the Moon

NASA’s Artemis II astronauts are go for launch, with the agency targeting April 1, 2026, at 18:24 local…...

Artemis II's 2026 launch window represents a critical 3-year delay from original 2024 plans, testing NASA's ability to execute sustained lunar operations amid budget pressures and technical complexity.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 30, 2026

China’s Y-9FQ Next Gen ASW Aircraft Intercepted for the First Time Over East China Sea

The Y-9FQ ‘High New 15’ made its debut at the Sep. 3, 2025, Victory Day parade in Beijing, and has now been intercepted for the first time by Japan. A Chinese People’s Liberation Army Naval Air Force (PLANAF) Y-9FQ maritime patrol and anti-submarine warfare aircraft was intercepted for the first...

China's newly unveiled Y-9FQ ASW aircraft is now operationally deployed in contested waters, signaling rapid fielding of advanced maritime patrol capabilities in the East China Sea.

See The Aviationist →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must systematically document why selected risk controls are acceptable when residual risk remains, creating an auditable decision trail for regulatory compliance.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Air Canada CEO Announces Retirement Following Criticism Over LaGuardia Crash Comments

Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau's retirement announcement comes after public backlash over remarks perceived as insensitive to a LaGuardia accident.

Executive leadership transitions driven by public relations crises underscore how airline reputational damage can accelerate C-suite turnover regardless of operational performance.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Why Pilots Sometimes Land Hard On Purpose

A harder landing often feels dramatic, but it is frequently the safer landing.

Steep descent angles and firm touchdowns can reduce landing distance and crosswind exposure, making them tactically superior to shallow approaches in challenging conditions.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

American’s BBQ partnership with Pecan Lodge hits the spot

Some airline routes are special because of the insatiable passenger demand they create, like Seoul to Jeju in South Korea, which is often ranked as the world’s busiest route. Others are special because they link two globally influential cities like New York and London. Then there’s Dallas to New...

Airlines are leveraging local culinary partnerships to differentiate route experiences and drive passenger loyalty beyond traditional hub-and-spoke advantages.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Varda flies navigation payload, heat shield tests on sixth reentry mission

Rhea Space, Sandia and NASA hardware to gather data during high-speed descent...

Varda's reentry testing partnership with NASA and Sandia validates critical navigation and thermal protection technologies essential for routine space manufacturing operations.

PAI
Safety Mar 30, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to identifying and mitigating risks before incidents happen, fundamentally changing how operators approach safety culture.

Military Mar 30, 2026

NORAD Escorts Aircraft After Palm Beach TFR Violation

F-16s intercepted the general aviation aircraft near Mar-a-Lago on Sunday afternoon.

TFR violations near high-security sites now trigger immediate military interception, raising awareness that airspace enforcement extends beyond major airports to protected facilities.

Sendoff for Artemis II Crew

From left to right, NASA astronauts Andre Douglas, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronauts Jenni Gibbons, NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen pose for a photo before the Artemis II crew proceed to a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas...

Artemis II's international crew composition demonstrates NASA's strategic emphasis on multinational collaboration for lunar exploration missions.

Only 27% Full: Avelo Airlines' 10 Emptiest Routes Revealed

Discover the shocking low load factors on Avelo Airlines' emptiest routes, but there's a twist...

Avelo's severely underutilized regional routes suggest either aggressive network expansion ahead of demand or potential capacity right-sizing opportunities for the carrier.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

How Airline Cabins Have Changed Since 2000

The year 2000 marked a turning point in airline cabin design. How far have these designs come in the years since?

Modern cabin innovations now prioritize passenger health and connectivity over mere comfort, reflecting post-pandemic safety standards and digital expectations that fundamentally reshape airline competitiveness.

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model balancing flight training, general aviation, and commercial carriers demonstrates a sustainable mid-size airport strategy for regional markets.

Air Algérie orders 10 Boeing 737-8s as fleet renewal accelerates

Air Algérie has ordered 10 Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft, with deliveries set to begin in summer 2026 as the…...

North African carriers' accelerating MAX orders signal growing confidence in Boeing's narrowbody despite prior certification delays and regional competition from Airbus.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

The Widebody Boeing Aircraft That Seats More Passengers Than Its Airbus Competitor

This aviation marvel that combines power, efficiency, and record-breaking seating in one sleek package.

Boeing's widebody configuration advantage in passenger density offers carriers higher revenue-per-flight potential compared to equivalent Airbus models.

Inside The Boeing 737 MAX's Flight Control System

How pilots fly the Boeing 737 MAX.

Understanding the 737 MAX's flight control architecture is essential for pilots to recognize automation boundaries and manual override procedures that differ from legacy 737 variants.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer

MILAN — The European Space Agency has launched the first two satellites of the Celeste in-orbit demonstration mission from New Zealand aboard a Rocket Lab Electron launcher on March 28. The two satellites were launched at 10:14 CET and separated from the launcher about an hour later into a...

ESA's Celeste LEO constellation will provide independent positioning backup to GPS, reducing aviation's reliance on single-source satellite navigation systems.

See SpaceNews →

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...

Regional airports like Frederick Municipal serve as crucial economic anchors by supporting diverse aviation activities from flight training to corporate operations within underserved markets.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission

SpaceX launched the latest in its series of dedicated rideshare missions March 30, delivering more than 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

SpaceX's rideshare model demonstrates commercial viability of smallsat deployment, enabling cost-effective orbital access for hundreds of operators annually.

See SpaceNews →

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 30, 2026

Formation flying with a friend's Subaru-powered home built.

Subaru automotive engines continue enabling cost-effective experimental aircraft construction, demonstrating viable alternatives to traditional aircraft powerplants for homebuilders.

Military Mar 30, 2026

5 Largest Aircraft Fleets Operated By The US Air Force

From F-16 fighters to KC-135 tankers, these five aircraft types make up the largest fleets in the US Air Force in 2026.

The USAF's fleet composition remains heavily dependent on legacy platforms like the KC-135 tanker, highlighting critical modernization gaps in aerial refueling capabilities.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Merlin Labs sees incremental path to pilot-free passenger flights

US start-up Merlin Labs is eyeing civil applications for its AI-powered, defense-grade Merlin Pilot autonomous flight control system and says there is “no hard ceiling” for the size of aircraft its technology could eventually be installed on. Describing fully uncrewed civil flight as “a very long...

Merlin Labs' scalable AI autonomy platform could eventually enable unpiloted operations on aircraft of any size, positioning autonomous flight as evolutionary rather than revolutionary for commercial aviation.

See Runway Girl Network →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers immediate emissions reductions without requiring new aircraft technology deployment.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Bars U.S. Iran-Related Military Flights From Its Airspace

Madrid expands earlier restrictions on base access for U.S. military flights.

Spain's escalating airspace restrictions signal how geopolitical tensions can fragment transatlantic military logistics networks and force operational rerouting.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit

Chinese firm CAS Space successfully reached orbit with the first launch of its Kinetica-2 launch vehicle Monday, with a prototype cargo spacecraft among the payloads.

China's successful Kinetica-2 debut expands the competitive landscape for commercial space logistics, signaling increased payload capacity options for international aerospace supply chains.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Airbus Faces Persistent Engine Shortages Threatening Future Aircraft Deliveries

The European planemaker is gearing up for its AGM next month.

Engine supply constraints are becoming a structural bottleneck that could limit Airbus's ability to meet production ramp targets even as demand remains strong.

Private sector involvement in air defense demonstrates how commercial aviation expertise and resources can be rapidly mobilized for critical national security operations.

Traffic Resumes After Potomac TRACON Evacuation

Ground stop affected Washington-area airports Friday for the second time in two weeks.

Repeated TRACON evacuations suggest potential systemic issues with Washington airspace infrastructure that could impact operational reliability for the busiest regional hub.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Press Release: The New Terminal One at JFK selects PRA services vendor

The New Terminal One at JFK has selected Wilson James, a leading provider of Passengers Requiring Assistance (PRA) services, as its dedicated partner to design and operate a comprehensive assistance program for passengers who require additional support when traveling through the new...

Terminal One's PRA partnership signals that next-generation airport infrastructure is prioritizing accessibility services as a core operational component rather than an afterthought, setting potential industry standards.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Emirates Pulls The Airbus A380 From 15 Major Routes: Here's Why

The continuing war in Iran has led to a drastic reduction in Emirates' A380 departures from Dubai, with plans subject to rapid change.

Geopolitical instability is forcing airlines to reassess aircraft deployment strategies, with wide-body redeployments creating cascading impacts on route profitability and fleet utilization metrics.

FBI says there is no continuing threat after passenger’s verbal bomb-threat.

Pilatus's PC-24 order signals growing demand for versatile twin-engine jets in defense and humanitarian operations across Southeast Asia's developing military infrastructure.

Airbus demonstrated autonomous air-to-air combat capability, marking a significant shift toward unmanned systems handling tactical engagements without human intervention.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Continues to Deny Airspace to U.S. Aircraft Involved with Operation Epic Fury

Spanish newspaper El País reported further on earlier news regarding Spain’s position on U.S. military action against Iran, with the country’s Prime Minister confirming all related flight plans have been rejected. After a mass exodus of U.S. Air Force (USAF) aerial refueling aircraft from Spanish...

Spain's airspace denial to U.S. tanker operations could disrupt aerial refueling logistics for Middle East operations, forcing costly routing alternatives.
